artists.name: Michelangelo
artists.intro: Thirty curated works and ensembles across eighteen places. Use the map to find where the originals are held today.
artists.tripSummary: Florence and Rome contain the largest concentrations, with original works also in London, Paris, Bruges, Milan, New York and elsewhere.

artists.name: Johannes Vermeer
artists.intro: Thirty-five curated paintings across collections in Europe, the United States and Japan.
artists.tripSummary: The Hague and Amsterdam are natural starting points, with important groups also in New York, Washington, London, Berlin and Dresden.

artists.name: Rembrandt
artists.intro: 742 works across 174 mapped collections. Check individual records against the linked source before travelling.
artists.tripSummary: This broad catalogue reveals concentrations across Dutch, European and American collections; verify individual works against the linked source.

artists.name: Claude Monet
artists.intro: 776 works across 217 mapped collections. Check individual records against the linked source before travelling.
artists.tripSummary: Paris is the largest starting point, with substantial holdings distributed across Europe, North America and Japan.

artists.name: Artemisia Gentileschi
artists.intro: 68 works across 48 mapped collections. Check individual records against the linked source before travelling.
artists.tripSummary: Her surviving works are widely dispersed, making this artist-centred view particularly useful for planning visits.
